summary: The team is racing to deliver the 'New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' joint report with IE by early September for Singapore launch events, while simultaneously ramping up the Intelligent China program and executing the Asia Start campaign with an A/B test on AI vs traditional routes.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the joint report with IE on Chinese companies going global to Southeast Asia, with a working title 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'. This week, Felix must meet with Tech in Asia and Tyson to confirm event partnerships for the September launch, Julie needs to translate the Chinese report into English, and the team must identify op-ed contributors from both schools. Meanwhile, the Intelligent China program targeting foreigners is launching with a focus on Module One in Beijing; Joseph needs to get the website ready and the team should start SCM campaigns. The Asia Start campaign is in its first month with an A/B test: Joseph using AI and Daisy using traditional strategy, after which one route will be selected. Additionally, Ira is finalizing an article titled 'China's Gateway' on behalf of the Dean and preparing the joint report coordination with Professor Li Wei and Felix. Other urgent items include reviewing Professor Li Wei's FT Chinese piece for potential repurposing and finalizing the press release on Sun Tianshu at Summer Davos 2026.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, titled 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', to be launched in Singapore in September 2026.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with focus on module one in Beijing.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation and Market Dynamics
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

actionItems.text: Meet with Tech in Asia tomorrow to confirm event theme and possibility of launching report there.
actionItems.detail: Tech in Asia event on Sep 15-16; need to align theme with report topic.

actionItems.text: Meet with Tyson tomorrow to discuss report launch at their event on Sep 29-30.
actionItems.detail: Tyson event is another potential launch venue; both events in Singapore.

actionItems.text: Translate the bulk of the Chinese report into English and share with team for review.
actionItems.detail: Core research from Professor Leeway needs translation.

actionItems.text: Identify and propose op-ed contributors from CKGSB (President Santiago, Mapping) and IE (Dean Hi How).
actionItems.detail: Op-eds to complement the main report; Mapping to find a way to comment.

actionItems.text: Look into adding industry perspective via case studies from the report or an op-ed from an alum active in Southeast Asia (e.g., To Tian).
actionItems.detail: Could summarize existing case studies or get alum contribution.

actionItems.text: Search WeChat official accounts for alumni case studies on going global that can be translated for the report.
actionItems.detail: Quick approval since content already exists.

actionItems.text: Create a one-pager proposal with report structure, title, and promotion plan for President Santiago.
actionItems.detail: To be finished by tomorrow or early next week.

actionItems.text: Get website for Intelligent China program ready
actionItems.detail: Website is nearly ready and needs to be launched.

actionItems.text: Start SCM campaign for Intelligent China program
actionItems.detail: Once webpage is done, begin campaign planning; decide on AI or traditional route.

actionItems.text: Revise article on 'Disruptive Timing' paper based on Professor Zhu Yang's feedback on title and opening
actionItems.meetingTitle: Re: News Article on 'Disruptive Timing' Paper
actionItems.detail: Professor Zhu suggested sharper title and opening for the article.

actionItems.text: Finalize article on behalf of Dean with chosen title 'China's Gateway' and coordinate with Shouhuo for Dean's confirmation
actionItems.meetingTitle: Re: Article on Behalf of Dean
actionItems.detail: Julie updated the article and suggested using the title with 'China's gateway'; Ira will reach out to Shouhuo for Dean's confirmation.

actionItems.text: Prepare joint report on Chinese companies going global with IE, coordinate with Professor Li Wei and Felix
actionItems.detail: Ira put together a plan for the joint report; Professor Li Wei agreed to collaborate; need to finalize sections and send to Felix.

actionItems.text: Review Professor Li Wei's FT Chinese piece for overlap with China Going Global report and consider repurposing
actionItems.meetingTitle: RE: 长江商学院媒体监测日报2026-6-25
actionItems.detail: Joseph will check the FT Chinese piece for new framing or substantial differences.

actionItems.text: Translate the media monitoring report item flagged by Ira
actionItems.meetingTitle: Re: 长江商学院媒体监测日报2026-6-25
actionItems.detail: Joseph offered to translate the item from the daily media monitoring report.

actionItems.text: Update team dashboard with new link
actionItems.detail: Joseph provided a corrected link for the team dashboard.

actionItems.text: Attend farewell reception for Mr. Martin Matter on July 1
actionItems.meetingTitle: FW: [Invitation] Farewell Reception for Mr. Martin Matter
actionItems.detail: Ira plans to attend and asked if others want to join.

actionItems.text: Review final version of press release on Sun Tianshu at Summer Davos 2026
actionItems.meetingTitle: RE: press release - Sun Tianshu at Summer Davos 2026
actionItems.detail: Joseph will review the final version after Ira's edits.

decisions.text: Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, jointly distributed.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report to be ready by early September 2026 for Singapore events.
Report will include a main research feature from Professor Leeway, plus op-eds from both schools and possibly industry case studies.
Asia Start first month will run two routes: Joseph using AI and Daisy using traditional strategy, then decide on one for rest of campaign.
Use the title 'China's Gateway' for the article on behalf of Dean
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: Article on Behalf of Dean

decisions.text: Professor Li Wei agreed to collaborate on the joint report with IE on Chinese companies going global
